name: Segmented Platform Pattern
markdown_content:
# AB-PAT-001

| Field              | Value                                                  |
|--------------------|--------------------------------------------------------|
| **ID**             | AB-PAT-001                                             |
| **Pattern Name**   | Segmented Platform Pattern                             |
| **Pattern ID**     | PAT-001                                                |
| **Version**        | 0.1                                                    |
| **Status**         | DRAFT                                                  |
| **Category**       | Structural                                             |
| **Owner**          | Enterprise Architecture                                |
| **Governance**     | Architecture Review Board, AI Technical Policy Committee |

---

## Intent

Partition the AI platform into three planes — Experience, Control, Execution — with a strict boundary rule that ensures governance, developer experience, and vendor-pluggable execution can evolve independently without coupling.

---

## Problem

Enterprise AI platforms face a structural tension: governance must be stable and non-negotiable, developer experience must evolve rapidly for adoption, and execution technologies change with every vendor release. Without structural separation, changes to governance slow down execution, changes to vendor technology break developer workflows, and shadow AI solutions proliferate because teams bypass the platform to move faster.

---

## Solution

Segment the platform into three planes. Each plane has a distinct responsibility, distinct ownership, and a distinct evolution cadence.

---

## Structural Elements

| Plane               | Responsibility                                                                                           | What Lives Here                                                                                                          |
|---------------------|----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| **Experience Plane** | How teams consume the platform — the enterprise front door that makes the platform reusable and easy to adopt | Developer portal, self-service templates, SDKs/CLIs, AI Capability Registry (consumption interface), Portable Agent Spec scaffolding |
| **Control Plane**    | How the platform governs — non-negotiable enterprise governance owned end-to-end                         | Identity & tenanting, policy/guardrails, agent & prompt registry, evaluation gates, observability & FinOps, secrets management |
| **Execution Plane**  | How AI workloads run — pluggable execution where vendors and technologies are interchangeable             | Model inference, retrieval/knowledge, agent runtimes, agent builders, domain platforms, enterprise tool/action services   |

---

## Invariants

**Control decides and governs. Execution executes. Experience never bypasses Control to reach Execution.**

This is the single non-negotiable invariant:

- If it establishes policy, identity boundaries, approvals, required logging/evals, or cost attribution — it belongs in the **Control Plane**
- If it runs inference, retrieval, orchestration, or actions — it belongs in the **Execution Plane**
- If it helps teams discover, build, deploy, or monitor capabilities — it belongs in the **Experience Plane**

Any attempt to create direct Experience-to-Execution paths that bypass Control Plane governance is an architecture violation requiring ARB exception.

---

## Inter-Element Contracts

| Contract                       | What It Governs                                                                                                                                                                      |
|--------------------------------|--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| **Experience ↔ Control**       | All consumer requests flow through Control Plane services. Authentication, agent lifecycle, rate limiting, and observability are mediated here. Experience can only expose what Control authorizes. |
| **Control ↔ Execution**        | All LLM calls route through the Model Gateway. All tool calls route through the Tool/Action Gateway. No production deployment without registry entry, evaluation gates, and telemetry. This is the most critical contract. |
| **Experience ↔ Execution**     | **Prohibited.** No direct contract exists. All interactions are mediated by the Control Plane.                                                                                        |

---

## Related Patterns

| Pattern  | Relationship                                                                                           |
|----------|--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| PAT-002  | Platform Integration Pattern — defines how the Execution Plane integrates with vendors through the Socket → Adapter → Service API chain. Operates *within* the structure defined by this blueprint. |

---

## Related ADRs

| ADR      | Decision                        |
|----------|---------------------------------|
| ADR-000  | Three-plane partitioning        |
| ADR-001  | AI Account scope                |
| ADR-006  | Single LLM Gateway              |

---

## Note on Building Blocks

This blueprint does not define Architecture Building Blocks (ABBs). The Segmented Platform is not a building block you compose into something — it is the structure itself. You do not plug it in, swap it, or reuse it across different contexts. It is the foundation everything else sits on. ABBs (such as those defined in PAT-002: Platform Integration Pattern) operate *within* this structure. The ABBs and SBBs for each plane are defined in the Platform Architecture document.

name: Agent
markdown_content:
# ABB-AGT-001

| Field       | Value                        |
|-------------|------------------------------|
| **ID**      | ABB-AGT-001                  |
| **Name**    | Agent                        |
| **Version** | 1.0.0                        |
| **Status**  | DRAFT                        |
| **Category**| Core AI/LLM > Agents         |
| **Owner**   | Enterprise AI Architect      |

---

## Functionality

Autonomous AI agents with reasoning, planning, and tool execution capabilities. Agents combine LLM intelligence with action execution to accomplish multi-step goals without continuous human direction. Includes:

- Reasoning and planning — ReAct, Chain-of-Thought, tree-of-thought, and plan-then-execute patterns
- Tool/function calling — structured invocation of external tools, APIs, and enterprise services via Tool Gateway (ABB-INTG-002)
- Memory management — short-term (conversation context), long-term (persistent knowledge), and working memory (task state)
- Multi-step task execution — decompose complex goals into sub-tasks, execute sequentially or in parallel, and handle failures with retry/fallback
- Multi-agent coordination — supervisor, peer, and hierarchical delegation patterns for complex workflows
- Guardrail integration — all agent actions subject to AI Guardrails (ABB-XCUT-001) for safety enforcement

---

## Interfaces

- **Inbound:** Agent invocation API — accepts goal description, context, constraints, and session ID
- **Outbound:** Model Gateway (ABB-INTG-001) for LLM reasoning; Tool Gateway (ABB-INTG-002) for action execution; Knowledge & Retrieval (ABB-KR-001) for information access

---

## Interoperability

| Relationship    | References                                                                                   |
|-----------------|----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| **Consumed by** | Experience Plane consumers — developer portal, self-service templates, domain applications    |
| **Works with**  | Model Gateway (ABB-INTG-001), Tool Gateway (ABB-INTG-002), Knowledge & Retrieval (ABB-KR-001), Prompt Engineering (ABB-CORE-001), AI Guardrails (ABB-XCUT-001) |

---

## Dependencies

- ABB-CORE-001 (Prompt Engineering) — agents use prompts for reasoning
- ABB-INTG-001 (Model Gateway) — agents require LLM access for planning and decision-making
- ABB-INTG-002 (Tool Gateway) — agents require tool access for action execution

---

## Business Capabilities

- Intelligent Automation
- Customer Service Agents
- Process Automation
- Decision Support
- Autonomous Workflow Execution

name: Prompt Engineering
markdown_content:
# ABB-CORE-001

| Field       | Value                           |
|-------------|---------------------------------|
| **ID**      | ABB-CORE-001                    |
| **Name**    | Prompt Engineering              |
| **Version** | 1.0.0                           |
| **Status**  | DRAFT                           |
| **Category**| Core AI/LLM > Foundational      |
| **Owner**   | Enterprise AI Architect         |

---

## Functionality

Design, compose, optimize, version, and deploy prompts for LLM interaction. This is the foundational capability that every AI workload depends on — any service that calls an LLM uses Prompt Engineering. Includes:

- Prompt design and composition — structured templates with variable injection, system/user/assistant role management, and few-shot example embedding
- Prompt versioning and lifecycle — version-controlled prompt definitions with promotion gates (dev → staging → production)
- Prompt optimization — iterative refinement through evaluation metrics (accuracy, latency, cost), A/B testing, and regression detection
- Output parsing and structured extraction — transform LLM responses into typed schemas (JSON, XML, domain objects)
- Prompt chaining and orchestration — multi-step prompt sequences where output of one prompt feeds the next

---

## Interfaces

- **Inbound:** Prompt execution API — accepts prompt template ID, input variables, model selection, and execution parameters
- **Outbound:** Model Gateway (ABB-INTG-001) — all LLM invocations route through the gateway; AI Guardrails (ABB-XCUT-001) — input/output safety enforcement

---

## Interoperability

| Relationship    | References                                                                                      |
|-----------------|-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| **Consumed by** | Knowledge & Retrieval (ABB-KR-001), Agents (ABB-AGT-001)                                       |
| **Works with**  | Model Gateway (ABB-INTG-001), AI Guardrails (ABB-XCUT-001)                                     |

---

## Dependencies

- ABB-INTG-001 (Model Gateway) — all prompt execution requires LLM access

---

## Business Capabilities

- Natural Language Interaction
- Content Generation
- Text Understanding
- Information Extraction

name: Model Gateway
markdown_content:
# ABB-INTG-001

| Field       | Value                        |
|-------------|------------------------------|
| **ID**      | ABB-INTG-001                 |
| **Name**    | Model Gateway                |
| **Version** | 1.0.0                        |
| **Status**  | DRAFT                        |
| **Category**| Integration                  |
| **Owner**   | Enterprise AI Architect      |

---

## Functionality

Unified access point for all AI/ML model invocations across the platform. No consumer calls a model directly — all inference traffic routes through the Model Gateway. This is the enforcement point for ADR-006 (Single LLM Gateway). Includes:

- LLM invocation — text generation, chat completion, streaming responses
- Embedding generation — text-to-vector conversion for semantic search and retrieval
- ML model inference — classification, prediction, and custom model endpoints
- Model routing and selection — direct traffic to the appropriate provider based on model ID, cost, latency, or capability
- Rate limiting and quota management — per-tenant, per-model throttling aligned with FinOps policy
- Failover and retry logic — automatic fallback across providers when primary is unavailable
- Usage tracking and cost attribution — per-request metering with tenant and project tagging for chargeback

---

## Interfaces

- **Inbound:** Unified model invocation API — accepts model ID, prompt/input, parameters (temperature, max_tokens), and tenant context
- **Outbound:** Routes to model provider SBBs (Bedrock, self-hosted, external) via adapters (ABB-PIP-002)

---

## Interoperability

| Relationship    | References                                                                                              |
|-----------------|---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| **Consumed by** | Prompt Engineering (ABB-CORE-001), Knowledge & Retrieval (ABB-KR-001), Agents (ABB-AGT-001)            |
| **Works with**  | AI Guardrails (ABB-XCUT-001), Socket Contract (ABB-PIP-001), Adapter (ABB-PIP-002)                     |

---

## Dependencies

- ABB-PIP-001 (Socket Contract) — defines the interface each model adapter must implement
- ABB-PIP-002 (Adapter) — translates socket contract to vendor-specific APIs

---

## Business Capabilities

- AI/ML Model Access
- Multi-Model Strategy
- Cost Governance and FinOps
- Vendor Portability

name: Tool Gateway
markdown_content:
# ABB-INTG-002

| Field       | Value                        |
|-------------|------------------------------|
| **ID**      | ABB-INTG-002                 |
| **Name**    | Tool Gateway                 |
| **Version** | 1.0.0                        |
| **Status**  | DRAFT                        |
| **Category**| Integration                  |
| **Owner**   | Enterprise AI Architect      |

---

## Functionality

Centralized tool and action integration layer for AI agents. Provides a uniform protocol for agents to discover, invoke, and interact with enterprise services, APIs, and data sources. Includes:

- Tool registration and discovery — centralized registry of available tools with metadata (name, description, input/output schema, permissions)
- MCP (Model Context Protocol) implementation — standard protocol for agent-to-tool communication
- API-to-MCP conversion — expose existing REST/GraphQL APIs as MCP-compatible tools without rewriting them
- Tool authorization and access control — enforce which agents/tenants can invoke which tools, integrated with Control Plane identity
- Tool execution monitoring — logging, tracing, and audit trail for every tool invocation
- Error handling and timeout management — graceful failure, retry, and fallback for tool calls

---

## Interfaces

- **Inbound:** MCP protocol — agents discover and invoke tools via standardized MCP messages
- **Outbound:** Enterprise APIs, Lambda functions, database connections, third-party services

---

## Interoperability

| Relationship    | References                                                          |
|-----------------|---------------------------------------------------------------------|
| **Consumed by** | Agents (ABB-AGT-001)                                               |
| **Works with**  | AI Guardrails (ABB-XCUT-001), Model Gateway (ABB-INTG-001)        |

---

## Dependencies

None

---

## Business Capabilities

- Tool Integration
- Enterprise Connectivity
- API Management
- Agent Action Governance

name: Knowledge & Retrieval
markdown_content:
# ABB-KR-001

| Field       | Value                              |
|-------------|------------------------------------|
| **ID**      | ABB-KR-001                         |
| **Name**    | Knowledge & Retrieval              |
| **Version** | 1.0.0                              |
| **Status**  | DRAFT                              |
| **Category**| Core AI/LLM > Knowledge & Retrieval|
| **Owner**   | Enterprise AI Architect            |

---

## Functionality

Retrieve and augment LLM responses with external knowledge. Implements Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) and enterprise search patterns to ground AI responses in authoritative data. Includes:

- Document ingestion — ingest from S3, SharePoint, databases, APIs, and file systems with format detection (PDF, DOCX, HTML, CSV)
- Chunking strategies — fixed-size, semantic, recursive, and document-structure-aware chunking with overlap control
- Embedding generation — convert chunks to vector representations via Model Gateway (ABB-INTG-001)
- Vector and semantic search — nearest-neighbor retrieval from vector stores with configurable similarity metrics
- Hybrid search — combine vector similarity with keyword/BM25 scoring for improved recall and precision
- Context assembly and ranking — rerank retrieved chunks by relevance, recency, and source authority before injecting into prompt
- Source attribution and citation — track provenance from chunk back to source document, page, and paragraph

---

## Interfaces

- **Inbound:** Retrieval API — accepts query text, filters (source, date range, metadata), and top-k parameter
- **Outbound:** Model Gateway (ABB-INTG-001) for embedding generation; vector stores and search indices for retrieval

---

## Interoperability

| Relationship    | References                                                                    |
|-----------------|-------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| **Consumed by** | Agents (ABB-AGT-001), Prompt Engineering (ABB-CORE-001)                      |
| **Works with**  | Model Gateway (ABB-INTG-001), AI Guardrails (ABB-XCUT-001)                   |

---

## Dependencies

- ABB-INTG-001 (Model Gateway) — embedding generation requires model access

---

## Business Capabilities

- Document Summarization
- Chat with Documents
- Enterprise Knowledge Search
- Intelligent Q&A
- Regulatory Document Analysis

name: Socket Contract
markdown_content:
# ABB-PIP-001

| Field       | Value                           |
|-------------|---------------------------------|
| **ID**      | ABB-PIP-001                     |
| **Name**    | Socket Contract                 |
| **Version** | 1.0.0                           |
| **Status**  | DRAFT                           |
| **Category**| Platform Integration Pattern    |
| **Owner**   | Enterprise AI Architect         |

---

## Functionality

Platform-defined interface contract that defines what any adapter must implement, independent of vendor technology. Includes:

- Input/output schema definition and data format standardization
- SLO expectations (latency, throughput, availability)
- Error handling and failure mode contracts
- Versioning and backward compatibility rules
- Required observability hooks (trace_id propagation, telemetry signals)

---

## Interfaces

Defined by ABB specification per capability category (CORE, INTG, AGT, XCUT)

---

## Interoperability

| Relationship    | References                                                          |
|-----------------|---------------------------------------------------------------------|
| **Consumed by** | Adapter (ABB-PIP-002), Service API Contract (ABB-PIP-003)          |
| **Works with**  | Observability (ABB-XCUT-001)                                       |

---

## Dependencies

None

---

## Business Capabilities

- Vendor pluggability — every integration begins with a socket contract

---

> 📦 **Deployment Note:** Deploys as part of the same service as ABB-PIP-003 (Service API Contract). Logically separate, physically composed.

name: Adapter
markdown_content:
# ABB-PIP-002

| Field       | Value                           |
|-------------|---------------------------------|
| **ID**      | ABB-PIP-002                     |
| **Name**    | Adapter                         |
| **Version** | 1.0.0                           |
| **Status**  | DRAFT                           |
| **Category**| Platform Integration Pattern    |
| **Owner**   | Enterprise AI Architect         |

---

## Functionality

Translation layer that maps a socket contract (ABB-PIP-001) to a specific vendor API. The only location where vendor-specific code resides. Includes:

- Socket-to-vendor API translation (request/response mapping)
- Vendor authentication and credential handling (via secrets management)
- Vendor-specific error mapping to platform-standard error codes
- SLO monitoring and reporting against socket contract expectations
- One adapter per vendor per socket — independently deployable and swappable

---

## Interfaces

Implements the interface defined by its corresponding socket contract (ABB-PIP-001)

---

## Interoperability

| Relationship    | References                                                          |
|-----------------|---------------------------------------------------------------------|
| **Consumed by** | Socket Contract (ABB-PIP-001)                                      |
| **Works with**  | Observability (ABB-XCUT-001)   |

---

## Dependencies

None

---

## Business Capabilities

- Vendor pluggability
- Technology swap without rewrite
- Multi-vendor strategy

---

> 📦 **Deployment Note:** Deploys with its corresponding SBB. Swap vendor = replace this adapter + SBB. Nothing above changes.

name: Service API Contract
markdown_content:
# ABB-PIP-003

| Field       | Value                           |
|-------------|---------------------------------|
| **ID**      | ABB-PIP-003                     |
| **Name**    | Service API Contract            |
| **Version** | 1.0.0                           |
| **Status**  | DRAFT                           |
| **Category**| Platform Integration Pattern    |
| **Owner**   | Enterprise AI Architect         |

---

## Functionality

Stable, versioned API surface that consumers call to access a platform capability. Shields consumers from all integration details — sockets, adapters, and vendors are invisible. Includes:

- Consumer-facing API definition (endpoints, request/response schemas, versioning)
- Rate limiting and throttling contracts
- Authentication and authorization integration with Control Plane
- API versioning and deprecation policy
- Consumer documentation and SDK generation

---

## Interfaces

Exposes capability to consumers; delegates to socket contracts (ABB-PIP-001) internally

---

## Interoperability

| Relationship    | References                                                          |
|-----------------|---------------------------------------------------------------------|
| **Consumed by** | PBCs, Agents, and all platform consumers                           |
| **Works with**  | Socket Contract (ABB-PIP-001), Observability (ABB-XCUT-001) |

---

## Dependencies

None

---

## Business Capabilities

- Stable consumption interface
- Developer experience
- API governance

---

> 📦 **Deployment Note:** Deploys as part of the same service as ABB-PIP-001 (Socket Contract). Consumers see only this layer. The socket and adapter mechanics are internal implementation details.

name: AI Guardrails
markdown_content:
# ABB-XCUT-001

| Field       | Value                        |
|-------------|------------------------------|
| **ID**      | ABB-XCUT-001                 |
| **Name**    | AI Guardrails                |
| **Version** | 1.0.0                        |
| **Status**  | DRAFT                        |
| **Category**| Cross-Cutting > Safety       |
| **Owner**   | Enterprise AI Architect      |

---

## Functionality

Input and output safety controls for all LLM interactions across the platform. Guardrails are a Control Plane concern — they enforce enterprise policy regardless of which model, runtime, or consumer is involved (ADR-007). Includes:

- Input validation — detect and block prompt injection attempts, PII leakage, and toxic input before it reaches the model
- Output filtering — intercept and sanitize harmful content, hallucinated claims, and policy-violating responses before they reach the consumer
- Policy enforcement — configurable topic denial lists, word filters, and custom content policies per tenant or use case
- Content moderation — sensitivity classification and escalation workflows for flagged content
- Guardrail versioning — version-controlled guardrail configurations with promotion gates aligned to prompt versioning
- Audit logging — every guardrail evaluation (pass/fail/override) is logged for compliance and incident response

---

## Interfaces

- **Integrated mode:** Guardrails applied inline during model invocation (requires native provider support)
- **Standalone mode:** Guardrails applied as a separate API call before/after model invocation (provider-agnostic)

---

## Interoperability

| Relationship    | References                                                                                                      |
|-----------------|-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| **Consumed by** | Prompt Engineering (ABB-CORE-001), Knowledge & Retrieval (ABB-KR-001), Agents (ABB-AGT-001), Model Gateway (ABB-INTG-001) |
| **Works with**  | Model Gateway (ABB-INTG-001), Prompt Engineering (ABB-CORE-001)                                                 |

---

## Dependencies

None

---

## Business Capabilities

- Responsible AI and Safety
- Regulatory Compliance
- Content Policy Enforcement
- PII Protection
- Audit and Accountability

name: Bedrock AgentCore
markdown_content:
# SBB-AGT-001

| Field       | Value                                   |
|-------------|-----------------------------------------|
| **ID**      | SBB-AGT-001                             |
| **Name**    | Bedrock AgentCore                       |
| **Version** | 1.0.0                                   |
| **Status**  | DRAFT                                   |
| **ABB Ref** | ABB-AGT-001 (Agent)                     |
| **Owner**   | Enterprise AI Architect                 |

---

## Specific Functionality

AWS managed agent service via Bedrock AgentCore. Fully managed runtime for agents that need built-in reasoning, tool use, and knowledge base integration without managing infrastructure. Includes:

- Fully managed agent runtime — AWS handles scaling, availability, and infrastructure; no EKS cluster management required
- Built-in reasoning and planning — ReAct-style agent loop with automatic thought → action → observation cycling
- Action groups for tool integration — define tools as Lambda functions or API Gateway endpoints; agent invokes them autonomously
- Knowledge base integration — native connection to Bedrock Knowledge Bases (SBB-KR-002) for RAG-augmented reasoning
- Session memory management — automatic conversation history and context window management across turns
- Multi-agent orchestration — supervisor and routing patterns with agent-to-agent delegation
- Native guardrail integration — Bedrock Guardrails (SBB-XCUT-001) applied to all agent LLM calls automatically

---

## Interfaces

- **Inbound:** Bedrock Agent InvokeAgent API — accepts session ID, input text, and optional context
- **Outbound:** AWS Bedrock (LLM), Lambda functions (tools), Bedrock Knowledge Bases (retrieval), S3 (code interpreter)

---

## Interoperability

| Relationship    | References                                                                                              |
|-----------------|---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| **Consumed by** | Experience Plane consumers — developer portal, domain applications, Step Functions workflows            |
| **Works with**  | AWS Bedrock (SBB-INTG-001), Bedrock Knowledge Bases (SBB-KR-002), AgentCore Gateway (SBB-INTG-007), Bedrock Guardrails (SBB-XCUT-001) |

---

## Dependent Building Blocks

- SBB-INTG-001 (AWS Bedrock) — LLM reasoning engine
- SBB-KR-002 (Bedrock Knowledge Bases) — retrieval-augmented reasoning (optional)
- SBB-INTG-007 (AgentCore Gateway) — MCP tool access (optional)

---

## SBB Mapping

- Runtime: AWS Managed (Bedrock AgentCore)
- LLM: AWS Bedrock (Claude, Titan, Llama)
- Tools: Lambda, API Gateway, AgentCore Gateway (MCP)
- Knowledge: Bedrock Knowledge Bases

name: Custom Agent Service
markdown_content:
# SBB-AGT-002

| Field       | Value                                   |
|-------------|-----------------------------------------|
| **ID**      | SBB-AGT-002                             |
| **Name**    | Custom Agent Service                    |
| **Version** | 1.0.0                                   |
| **Status**  | DRAFT                                   |
| **ABB Ref** | ABB-AGT-001 (Agent)                     |
| **Owner**   | Enterprise AI Architect                 |

---

## Specific Functionality

Self-hosted agent service on EKS using open-source frameworks. For workloads that require full control over agent logic, custom reasoning patterns, or multi-provider model access via the LLM Gateway. Includes:

- Full control over agent logic — custom reasoning loops, state machines, and orchestration patterns beyond what managed services offer
- Open-source framework support — LangGraph, CrewAI, AutoGen, Semantic Kernel, or custom-built agent frameworks
- MCP (Model Context Protocol) support — native MCP client for tool discovery and invocation via AgentCore Gateway (SBB-INTG-007) or Custom MCP Server (SBB-INTG-008)
- Multi-provider LLM access — route through LLM Gateway (SBB-INTG-003) for Bedrock, Azure OpenAI, or self-hosted models; or call Bedrock directly
- Custom memory backends — Redis, DynamoDB, PostgreSQL, or in-memory for conversation history and long-term agent memory
- Custom tool integration — REST APIs, GraphQL, database queries, file processing, and enterprise service calls
- Guardrail enforcement — ApplyGuardrail API (SBB-XCUT-002) for standalone safety checks on non-Bedrock models; or integrated guardrails for direct Bedrock calls

---

## Interfaces

- **Inbound:** REST/gRPC API — accepts goal, context, session ID, and execution parameters
- **Outbound:** LLM Gateway (SBB-INTG-003) or Bedrock direct (SBB-INTG-001) for reasoning; MCP servers (SBB-INTG-007, SBB-INTG-008) for tools; Knowledge & Retrieval (SBB-KR-001) for information

---

## Interoperability

| Relationship    | References                                                                                                       |
|-----------------|------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| **Consumed by** | Experience Plane consumers — developer portal, domain applications, workflow orchestrators                        |
| **Works with**  | LLM Gateway (SBB-INTG-003), AWS Bedrock (SBB-INTG-001), Custom RAG (SBB-KR-001), MCP servers (SBB-INTG-007, SBB-INTG-008), ApplyGuardrail API (SBB-XCUT-002) |

---

## Dependent Building Blocks

- SBB-INTG-003 (LLM Gateway / LiteLLM) or SBB-INTG-001 (AWS Bedrock) — LLM reasoning engine
- SBB-INTG-007 (AgentCore Gateway) or SBB-INTG-008 (Custom MCP Server) — tool access
- SBB-XCUT-002 (Bedrock ApplyGuardrail API) — standalone safety enforcement

---

## SBB Mapping

- Runtime: Amazon EKS
- Framework: LangGraph, CrewAI, AutoGen, or custom
- LLM: LLM Gateway (LiteLLM) or Bedrock direct
- Tools: MCP, REST APIs, Lambda
- Memory: Redis, DynamoDB, or PostgreSQL

name: Salesforce Agentforce
markdown_content:
# SBB-AGT-003

| Field       | Value                                   |
|-------------|-----------------------------------------|
| **ID**      | SBB-AGT-003                             |
| **Name**    | Salesforce Agentforce                   |
| **Version** | 1.0.0                                   |
| **Status**  | DRAFT                                   |
| **ABB Ref** | ABB-AGT-001 (Agent)                     |
| **Owner**   | Enterprise AI Architect                 |

---

## Specific Functionality

Salesforce managed agent platform for CRM-native AI agents. Operates within the Salesforce ecosystem with its own trust layer and data governance. Includes:

- Pre-built agents — ready-to-deploy agents for Sales (lead qualification, opportunity scoring), Service (case routing, resolution), and Marketing (campaign optimization, personalization)
- Native Salesforce data integration — direct access to Salesforce objects, records, and relationships without ETL; agents reason over live CRM data
- Einstein Trust Layer — Salesforce-native guardrails including PII masking, toxicity detection, and audit logging independent of platform guardrails (ABB-XCUT-001)
- Low-code agent builder — declarative agent configuration via Agent Builder UI; define topics, actions, and instructions without code
- MuleSoft API integration — connect agents to enterprise systems outside Salesforce via MuleSoft Anypoint connectors
- Grounding with Data Cloud — agents can access unified customer profiles across Salesforce and external data sources

---

## Interfaces

- **Inbound:** Salesforce UI (Lightning, Service Console), Salesforce APIs, Einstein Copilot
- **Outbound:** Salesforce objects, Apex classes, Flow automations, MuleSoft APIs

---

## Interoperability

| Relationship    | References                                                          |
|-----------------|---------------------------------------------------------------------|
| **Consumed by** | CRM, Sales, Service, Marketing applications, Slack integrations    |
| **Works with**  | Salesforce Data Cloud, MuleSoft, Salesforce Flow                    |

---

## Dependent Building Blocks

None — Salesforce SaaS (operates outside platform Execution Plane; governed by its own trust layer)

---

## SBB Mapping

- Runtime: Salesforce SaaS
- LLM: Einstein GPT, external models via Einstein Trust Layer
- Data: Salesforce objects, Data Cloud
- Tools: Apex, Flow, MuleSoft APIs

name: Microsoft Copilot Studio
markdown_content:
# SBB-AGT-004

| Field       | Value                                   |
|-------------|-----------------------------------------|
| **ID**      | SBB-AGT-004                             |
| **Name**    | Microsoft Copilot Studio                |
| **Version** | 1.0.0                                   |
| **Status**  | DRAFT                                   |
| **ABB Ref** | ABB-AGT-001 (Agent)                     |
| **Owner**   | Enterprise AI Architect                 |

---

## Specific Functionality

Microsoft managed agent platform for productivity and enterprise workflow automation within the Microsoft 365 ecosystem. Includes:

- Low-code/no-code agent builder — visual agent designer with topic-based conversation flows, generative AI answers, and custom plugin actions
- Microsoft 365 integration — agents operate natively within Teams, SharePoint, Outlook, and other M365 applications
- Teams, SharePoint, Outlook connectors — agents access organizational data, documents, emails, and calendar events via Microsoft Graph API
- Power Platform integration — connect agents to Power Automate flows for enterprise workflow automation and Dataverse for structured data
- Azure OpenAI backend — LLM reasoning powered by Azure OpenAI (GPT-4, GPT-4o) with enterprise data residency and compliance
- Generative AI plugin framework — extend agents with custom plugins, OpenAPI-based actions, and Microsoft Copilot extensibility model

---

## Interfaces

- **Inbound:** Microsoft Teams chat, SharePoint web parts, custom websites, Power Virtual Agents API
- **Outbound:** Microsoft Graph API, Power Automate, Dataverse, Azure OpenAI, custom connectors

---

## Interoperability

| Relationship    | References                                                          |
|-----------------|---------------------------------------------------------------------|
| **Consumed by** | Microsoft 365 users, Teams channels, internal productivity applications |
| **Works with**  | Power Automate, Dataverse, SharePoint, Microsoft Graph API          |

---

## Dependent Building Blocks

None — Microsoft SaaS (operates outside platform Execution Plane; governed by Microsoft responsible AI framework)

---

## SBB Mapping

- Runtime: Microsoft SaaS (Azure)
- LLM: Azure OpenAI (GPT-4, GPT-4o)
- Data: SharePoint, Dataverse, Microsoft Graph API
- Tools: Power Automate, custom connectors, Copilot plugins

name: Prompt Engineering Service (Direct — EKS)
markdown_content:
# SBB-CORE-001

| Field       | Value                                        |
|-------------|----------------------------------------------|
| **ID**      | SBB-CORE-001                                 |
| **Name**    | Prompt Engineering Service (Direct — EKS)    |
| **Version** | 1.0.0                                        |
| **Status**  | DRAFT                                        |
| **ABB Ref** | ABB-CORE-001 (Prompt Engineering)            |
| **Owner**   | Enterprise AI Architect                      |

---

## Specific Functionality

Prompt design, composition, optimization, versioning, and execution via direct AWS Bedrock API integration, deployed on Amazon EKS. This is the container-native variant for long-running, high-throughput workloads that call Bedrock directly without a gateway intermediary. Includes:

- Direct Bedrock InvokeModel / InvokeModelWithResponseStream API calls — lowest latency path to Bedrock models
- Prompt template management — version-controlled templates stored in configuration (S3, Parameter Store, or Git-backed)
- Inline guardrail enforcement — Bedrock guardrailIdentifier applied during model invocation (SBB-XCUT-001)
- Structured output parsing — extract JSON, entities, or classifications from model responses
- Observability integration — request/response logging, latency metrics, and token usage tracking via OpenTelemetry

---

## Interfaces

- **Inbound:** REST API — consumer applications invoke prompts by template ID with input variables
- **Outbound (LLM):** AWS Bedrock InvokeModel API (direct)
- **Outbound (Guardrails):** Bedrock Guardrails applied inline via guardrailIdentifier parameter

---

## Interoperability

| Relationship    | References                                                                      |
|-----------------|---------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| **Consumed by** | Agents (SBB-AGT-001, SBB-AGT-002), Knowledge & Retrieval (SBB-KR-001), domain applications |
| **Works with**  | Bedrock Guardrails — Integrated (SBB-XCUT-001), AWS Bedrock (SBB-INTG-001)    |

---

## Dependent Building Blocks

- SBB-INTG-001 (AWS Bedrock) — direct model invocation
- SBB-XCUT-001 (Bedrock Guardrails — Integrated) — inline safety enforcement

---

## SBB Mapping

- Runtime: Amazon EKS
- LLM: AWS Bedrock (direct)
- Guardrails: AWS Bedrock Guardrails (integrated)

name: Prompt Engineering Service (Gateway-Routed)
markdown_content:
# SBB-CORE-002

| Field       | Value                                          |
|-------------|------------------------------------------------|
| **ID**      | SBB-CORE-002                                   |
| **Name**    | Prompt Engineering Service (Gateway-Routed)    |
| **Version** | 1.0.0                                          |
| **Status**  | DRAFT                                          |
| **ABB Ref** | ABB-CORE-001 (Prompt Engineering)              |
| **Owner**   | Enterprise AI Architect                        |

---

## Specific Functionality

Prompt design, composition, optimization, versioning, and execution routed through the LLM Gateway (SBB-INTG-003 / LiteLLM) rather than directly to a model provider. This is the multi-model, multi-runtime variant of the Prompt Engineering Service. Includes:

- Prompt execution via LLM Gateway — supports any model behind LiteLLM (Bedrock, Azure OpenAI, self-hosted)
- Multi-runtime deployment — application runs on Amazon EKS, EC2, or Lambda depending on workload profile
- Guardrail enforcement via Bedrock ApplyGuardrail API (SBB-XCUT-002) — standalone mode, since guardrails cannot be applied inline when calls route through the gateway
- Model-agnostic prompt templating — prompts are authored against a standard schema and the gateway handles provider-specific translation
- Failover and model routing delegated to the gateway layer

---

## Interfaces

- **Inbound:** Consumer applications call the service via its API (REST/gRPC)
- **Outbound (LLM):** OpenAI-compatible API provided by SBB-INTG-003 (LiteLLM Gateway)
- **Outbound (Guardrails):** Bedrock ApplyGuardrail API — called before prompt submission (input validation) and after model response (output filtering)

---

## Interoperability

| Relationship    | References                                                                              |
|-----------------|-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| **Consumed by** | Agents (SBB-AGT-*), workflow services, and platform consumers requiring LLM interaction |
| **Works with**  | LLM Gateway (SBB-INTG-003), Standalone Guardrails (SBB-XCUT-002)                       |

---

## Dependent Building Blocks

- SBB-INTG-003 (LLM Gateway / LiteLLM) — all model invocations route through this gateway
- SBB-XCUT-002 (Bedrock ApplyGuardrail API) — input/output safety enforcement

---

## SBB Mapping

- Runtime: Amazon EKS, Amazon EC2, or AWS Lambda
- LLM Access: LLM Gateway (SBB-INTG-003 / LiteLLM)
- Guardrails: AWS Bedrock ApplyGuardrail API (standalone)

name: Prompt Engineering Service (Direct — Lambda)
markdown_content:
# SBB-CORE-003

| Field       | Value                                           |
|-------------|------------------------------------------------|
| **ID**      | SBB-CORE-003                                    |
| **Name**    | Prompt Engineering Service (Direct — Lambda)    |
| **Version** | 1.0.0                                           |
| **Status**  | DRAFT                                           |
| **ABB Ref** | ABB-CORE-001 (Prompt Engineering)               |
| **Owner**   | Enterprise AI Architect                         |

---

## Specific Functionality

Prompt design, composition, optimization, versioning, and execution via direct AWS Bedrock API integration, deployed on AWS Lambda. This is the serverless variant optimized for event-driven, bursty, or low-frequency workloads where container overhead is unjustified. Includes:

- Direct Bedrock InvokeModel API calls — serverless execution with pay-per-invocation cost model
- Event-driven triggering — invoked by API Gateway, SQS, EventBridge, S3, or Step Functions
- Inline guardrail enforcement — Bedrock guardrailIdentifier applied during model invocation (SBB-XCUT-001)
- Standalone guardrail support — Bedrock ApplyGuardrail API (SBB-XCUT-002) for pre/post validation when calling non-Bedrock models or when separate guardrail evaluation is required
- Cold start optimization — provisioned concurrency for latency-sensitive paths, SnapStart where available
- Prompt template management — templates loaded from S3 or Parameter Store at invocation time

---

## Interfaces

- **Inbound:** AWS API Gateway (REST/HTTP API), direct Lambda invocation, or event source mapping
- **Outbound (LLM):** AWS Bedrock InvokeModel API (direct)
- **Outbound (Guardrails):** Bedrock Guardrails inline (SBB-XCUT-001) and/or ApplyGuardrail API standalone (SBB-XCUT-002)

---

## Interoperability

| Relationship    | References                                                                         |
|-----------------|------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| **Consumed by** | Step Functions workflows, event-driven pipelines, lightweight API consumers        |
| **Works with**  | Bedrock Guardrails — Integrated (SBB-XCUT-001), ApplyGuardrail API (SBB-XCUT-002), AWS Bedrock (SBB-INTG-001) |

---

## Dependent Building Blocks

- SBB-INTG-001 (AWS Bedrock) — direct model invocation
- SBB-XCUT-001 (Bedrock Guardrails — Integrated) — inline safety enforcement
- SBB-XCUT-002 (Bedrock ApplyGuardrail API) — standalone safety enforcement (optional)

---

## SBB Mapping

- Runtime: AWS Lambda
- LLM: AWS Bedrock (direct)
- Guardrails: AWS Bedrock Guardrails (integrated + standalone)

name: AWS Bedrock
markdown_content:
# SBB-INTG-001

| Field       | Value                                   |
|-------------|-----------------------------------------|
| **ID**      | SBB-INTG-001                            |

| **Status**  | DRAFT                                   |

| **Owner**   | Enterprise AI Architect                 |

---

## Specific Functionality

AWS managed foundation model service providing access to leading LLMs and embedding models as a fully managed API. This is the primary model provider for the platform. Includes:

- Foundation model access — Claude (Anthropic), Titan (Amazon), Llama (Meta), Mistral, Cohere, and other models available on-demand
- Embedding models — Titan Embeddings, Cohere Embed for vector generation used by Knowledge & Retrieval (ABB-KR-001)
- Throughput modes — on-demand (pay-per-token) and provisioned throughput (reserved capacity for predictable workloads)
- Cross-region inference — route requests across AWS regions for availability and latency optimization
- Built-in security — VPC endpoints, IAM-based access control, encryption at rest and in transit, CloudTrail audit logging
- Native guardrail integration — Bedrock Guardrails applied inline during InvokeModel via guardrailIdentifier (SBB-XCUT-001)

---

## Interfaces

- **Inbound:** AWS Bedrock InvokeModel / InvokeModelWithResponseStream API, Converse API
- **Outbound:** Model responses returned to calling service

---

## Interoperability

| Relationship    | References                                                                                           |
|-----------------|------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| **Consumed by** | Prompt Engineering — Direct (SBB-CORE-001, SBB-CORE-003), LLM Gateway (SBB-INTG-003), Knowledge & Retrieval (SBB-KR-001, SBB-KR-002), Agents (SBB-AGT-001) |
| **Works with**  | Bedrock Guardrails — Integrated (SBB-XCUT-001)                                                      |

---

## Dependent Building Blocks

None — AWS managed service

---

## SBB Mapping

- Runtime: AWS Managed
- Models: Claude, Titan, Llama, Mistral, Cohere
- Embeddings: Titan Embeddings, Cohere Embed

name: LLM Invocation Service
markdown_content:
# SBB-INTG-002

| Field       | Value                                   |
|-------------|-----------------------------------------|
| **ID**      | SBB-INTG-002                            |
| **Name**    | LLM Invocation Service                  |
| **Version** | 1.0.0                                   |
| **Status**  | DRAFT                                   |
| **ABB Ref** | ABB-INTG-001 (Model Gateway)            |
| **Owner**   | Enterprise AI Architect                 |

---

## Specific Functionality

Custom-built wrapper service that provides a unified LLM invocation API for applications that need a thin abstraction layer over Bedrock without the full LiteLLM gateway. Deployed on EKS. Includes:

- Unified API — single endpoint that normalizes request/response formats across Bedrock model families (Claude, Titan, Llama have different API shapes)
- Request/response transformation — translate between application-specific schemas and Bedrock Converse API format
- Centralized logging and monitoring — structured request/response logging, token usage tracking, latency metrics via OpenTelemetry
- Tenant context propagation — inject tenant ID, project ID, and cost center into every request for FinOps attribution
- Retry and circuit breaker — automatic retry with exponential backoff, circuit breaker for model endpoint failures

---

## Interfaces

- **Inbound:** REST API — unified model invocation endpoint with model selection, prompt, and parameters
- **Outbound:** AWS Bedrock InvokeModel / Converse API

---

## Interoperability

| Relationship    | References                                                                   |
|-----------------|------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| **Consumed by** | Prompt Engineering (SBB-CORE-001, SBB-CORE-003), Agents (SBB-AGT-002), Knowledge & Retrieval (SBB-KR-001) |
| **Works with**  | AWS Bedrock (SBB-INTG-001), Bedrock Guardrails — Integrated (SBB-XCUT-001) |

---

## Dependent Building Blocks

- SBB-INTG-001 (AWS Bedrock) — backend model provider

---

## SBB Mapping

- Runtime: Amazon EKS
- LLM: AWS Bedrock (via Converse API)
- Observability: OpenTelemetry

name: LLM Gateway (LiteLLM)
markdown_content:
# SBB-INTG-003

| Field       | Value                                   |
|-------------|-----------------------------------------|
| **ID**      | SBB-INTG-003                            |
| **Name**    | LLM Gateway (LiteLLM)                   |
| **Version** | 1.0.0                                   |
| **Status**  | DRAFT                                   |
| **ABB Ref** | ABB-INTG-001 (Model Gateway)            |
| **Owner**   | Enterprise AI Architect                 |

---

## Specific Functionality

Self-hosted multi-provider LLM gateway using LiteLLM, deployed on EKS. This is the multi-model routing layer that abstracts provider differences behind a single OpenAI-compatible API. Includes:

- Multi-provider model routing — route requests to AWS Bedrock, Azure OpenAI, self-hosted models, and external APIs through a single endpoint
- OpenAI-compatible API — all consumers call the same API shape regardless of backend provider; enables model swap without consumer code changes
- Load balancing and failover — distribute traffic across model deployments, automatic failover when a provider is unavailable
- Model aliasing — map logical model names (e.g., "default-chat") to physical model IDs; swap models by changing config, not consumer code
- Rate limiting and quota management — per-key, per-model throttling with configurable limits
- Usage tracking and cost attribution — per-request token counting and cost calculation across all providers
- Request/response logging — structured audit log for compliance and debugging

---

## Interfaces

- **Inbound:** OpenAI-compatible REST API (chat/completions, embeddings, completions)
- **Outbound:** AWS Bedrock API, Azure OpenAI API, self-hosted model endpoints (vLLM, TGI), external provider APIs

---

## Interoperability

| Relationship    | References                                                                                    |
|-----------------|-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| **Consumed by** | Prompt Engineering — Gateway-Routed (SBB-CORE-002), Agents (SBB-AGT-002), Knowledge & Retrieval (SBB-KR-001) |
| **Works with**  | AWS Bedrock (SBB-INTG-001), Self-Hosted LLM/SLM (SBB-INTG-004), ApplyGuardrail API (SBB-XCUT-002) |

---

## Dependent Building Blocks

- SBB-INTG-001 (AWS Bedrock) — primary backend model provider
- SBB-INTG-004 (Self-Hosted LLM/SLM) — optional self-hosted backend models

---

## SBB Mapping

- Runtime: Amazon EKS
- Software: LiteLLM (open source)
- Backend Providers: AWS Bedrock, Azure OpenAI, self-hosted (vLLM, TGI)

name: Self-Hosted LLM/SLM
markdown_content:
# SBB-INTG-004

| Field       | Value                                   |
|-------------|-----------------------------------------|
| **ID**      | SBB-INTG-004                            |
| **Name**    | Self-Hosted LLM/SLM                     |
| **Version** | 1.0.0                                   |
| **Status**  | DRAFT                                   |
| **ABB Ref** | ABB-INTG-001 (Model Gateway)            |
| **Owner**   | Enterprise AI Architect                 |

---

## Specific Functionality

Self-hosted large and small language model serving on EKS or EC2 using open-source inference engines. For workloads requiring data sovereignty, custom fine-tuned models, or cost optimization on high-volume inference. Includes:

- Large Language Model serving — LLaMA, Mistral, Falcon, and other open-weight models served via vLLM or Text Generation Inference (TGI)
- Small Language Model serving — Phi, Gemma, Qwen, and other compact models for latency-sensitive or edge-adjacent workloads
- GPU resource management — NVIDIA GPU allocation, multi-GPU serving, and GPU sharing across model instances on EKS
- Model lifecycle — download from Hugging Face or Artifactory, load, warm up, health check, and rolling update without downtime
- Autoscaling — horizontal pod autoscaling based on request queue depth, GPU utilization, or custom metrics
- OpenAI-compatible API — vLLM/TGI expose an OpenAI-compatible endpoint consumed by the LLM Gateway (SBB-INTG-003)

---

## Interfaces

- **Inbound:** OpenAI-compatible REST API (served by vLLM/TGI)
- **Outbound:** None — this is the model execution layer

---

## Interoperability

| Relationship    | References                                                          |
|-----------------|---------------------------------------------------------------------|
| **Consumed by** | LLM Gateway (SBB-INTG-003), Prompt Engineering (SBB-CORE-002)     |
| **Works with**  | ApplyGuardrail API (SBB-XCUT-002)                                  |

---

## Dependent Building Blocks

None

---

## SBB Mapping

- Runtime: Amazon EKS or EC2 (GPU instances — p4d, p5, g5, g6)
- Inference Engine: vLLM or Text Generation Inference (TGI)
- Model Source: Hugging Face Hub, Artifactory

name: Self-Hosted Embedding Service
markdown_content:
# SBB-INTG-005

| Field       | Value                                   |
|-------------|-----------------------------------------|
| **ID**      | SBB-INTG-005                            |
| **Name**    | Self-Hosted Embedding Service           |
| **Version** | 1.0.0                                   |
| **Status**  | DRAFT                                   |
| **ABB Ref** | ABB-INTG-001 (Model Gateway)            |
| **Owner**   | Enterprise AI Architect                 |

---

## Specific Functionality

Self-hosted embedding model serving on EKS for high-throughput, low-latency vector generation. Used when Bedrock Titan Embeddings does not meet throughput, cost, or model selection requirements. Includes:

- Text embedding model serving — sentence-transformers, BGE, E5, GTE, and other open-source embedding models
- Batch and real-time modes — synchronous single-document embedding and asynchronous batch processing for large-scale ingestion
- High throughput, low latency — optimized inference with ONNX Runtime or TensorRT for sub-10ms per-document embedding
- Dimensionality configuration — support for various embedding dimensions (384, 768, 1024) aligned with vector store index configuration
- Model versioning — manage multiple embedding model versions simultaneously to support re-indexing workflows

---

## Interfaces

- **Inbound:** REST API — accepts text or text array, returns embedding vectors
- **Outbound:** None — this is the embedding execution layer

---

## Interoperability

| Relationship    | References                                                                         |
|-----------------|------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| **Consumed by** | Knowledge & Retrieval — Custom RAG (SBB-KR-001), LLM Gateway (SBB-INTG-003)      |
| **Works with**  | Vector stores (OpenSearch, Aurora pgvector)                                         |

---

## Dependent Building Blocks

None

---

## SBB Mapping

- Runtime: Amazon EKS (GPU or CPU instances depending on model size)
- Inference Engine: ONNX Runtime, TensorRT, or Hugging Face Transformers
- Model Source: Hugging Face Hub, Artifactory

Self-hosted traditional ML model serving on EKS or EC2. For classification, prediction, anomaly detection, and other non-LLM inference workloads that require full control over the inference environment. Includes:

- Custom ML model deployment — serve XGBoost, scikit-learn, PyTorch, TensorFlow, and ONNX models via REST API
- Custom pre/post-processing — data transformation, feature engineering, and output formatting pipelines co-deployed with the model
- Model versioning and A/B testing — serve multiple model versions simultaneously with traffic splitting for canary deployments
- Integration with Kubernetes infrastructure — leverage existing EKS cluster, Helm charts, and GitOps workflows
- Cost optimization — right-size instances for predictable CPU/memory-bound workloads without GPU overhead
- Health checks and auto-recovery — liveness/readiness probes with automatic pod restart on failure

name: AgentCore Gateway
markdown_content:
# SBB-INTG-007

| Field       | Value                                   |
|-------------|-----------------------------------------|
| **ID**      | SBB-INTG-007                            |
| **Name**    | AgentCore Gateway                       |
| **Version** | 1.0.0                                   |
| **Status**  | DRAFT                                   |
| **ABB Ref** | ABB-INTG-002 (Tool Gateway)             |
| **Owner**   | Enterprise AI Architect                 |

---

## Specific Functionality

AWS managed MCP (Model Context Protocol) server service via Bedrock AgentCore. Provides zero-code tool creation from existing APIs, enabling agents to discover and invoke enterprise services without custom integration code. Includes:

- Zero-code MCP tool creation — define tools declaratively from OpenAPI specs; no custom server code required
- REST API to MCP conversion — automatically expose existing REST APIs as MCP-compatible tools for agent consumption
- Intelligent tool discovery — agents query available tools by capability description; AgentCore returns matching tools ranked by relevance
- Serverless infrastructure — AWS-managed scaling, no cluster or capacity management required
- Built-in authentication — IAM-based tool access control with per-agent permission boundaries
- Audit logging — every tool invocation logged via CloudTrail for compliance

---

## Interfaces

- **Inbound:** MCP protocol (Streamable HTTP transport) — agents discover and invoke tools
- **Outbound:** Backend REST APIs, Lambda functions, API Gateway endpoints

---

## Interoperability

| Relationship    | References                                                          |
|-----------------|---------------------------------------------------------------------|
| **Consumed by** | Bedrock AgentCore (SBB-AGT-001), Custom Agents (SBB-AGT-002)      |
| **Works with**  | AWS Lambda, Amazon API Gateway                                      |

---

## Dependent Building Blocks

None — AWS managed service

---

## SBB Mapping

- Runtime: AWS Managed (Bedrock AgentCore)
- Protocol: MCP
- Transport: Streamable HTTP

name: Custom MCP Server

## Specific Functionality

Self-hosted MCP (Model Context Protocol) server on EKS for custom tool implementations that require full control over tool logic, data access, or processing. Used when AgentCore Gateway (SBB-INTG-007) cannot meet customization requirements. Includes:

- Custom tool implementation — full-code MCP tools with arbitrary logic, database access, file processing, or external service orchestration
- MCP protocol compliance — implements MCP server specification with tool listing, tool invocation, and resource exposure
- Multi-tool hosting — single MCP server instance can expose multiple tools, grouped by domain or capability
- Custom authentication — integrate with enterprise identity (OAuth, mTLS, API keys) beyond IAM-only access
- Stateful tool sessions — maintain session context across multiple tool invocations for multi-step workflows
- Sidecar deployment — can run as sidecar alongside agent pods on EKS for low-latency tool access

---

## Interfaces

- **Inbound:** MCP protocol (Streamable HTTP or SSE transport)
- **Outbound:** Enterprise databases, internal APIs, third-party services, file systems

## Dependent Building Blocks

None

---

## SBB Mapping

- Protocol: MCP

name: RAG Service (Custom)
markdown_content:
# SBB-KR-001

| Field       | Value                                       |
|-------------|---------------------------------------------|
| **ID**      | SBB-KR-001                                  |
| **Name**    | RAG Service (Custom)                        |
| **Version** | 1.0.0                                       |
| **Status**  | DRAFT                                       |
| **ABB Ref** | ABB-KR-001 (Knowledge & Retrieval)          |
| **Owner**   | Enterprise AI Architect                     |

---

## Specific Functionality

Custom-built RAG implementation deployed on EKS or Lambda. Full control over every stage of the retrieval-augmented generation pipeline — ingestion, chunking, embedding, indexing, retrieval, reranking, and context assembly. Includes:

- Document ingestion pipeline — ingest from S3, SharePoint, databases, APIs; support for PDF, DOCX, HTML, CSV, and custom formats with configurable parsers
- Chunking strategies — fixed-size, semantic, recursive, parent-child, and sliding-window chunking with configurable overlap
- Embedding generation — via Bedrock Titan Embeddings (SBB-INTG-001), self-hosted embedding models (SBB-INTG-005), or LLM Gateway (SBB-INTG-003)
- Vector store integration — index and retrieve from OpenSearch (k-NN), Aurora PostgreSQL (pgvector), or S3-based indices
- Hybrid search — combine vector similarity with keyword/BM25 scoring for improved recall; metadata filtering for scoped retrieval
- Reranking — cross-encoder reranking models to improve precision of top-k results before context injection
- Context assembly — assemble retrieved chunks into prompt context with source attribution, citation links, and confidence scoring
- Guardrail enforcement — input/output validation via Bedrock Guardrails (SBB-XCUT-001 integrated or SBB-XCUT-002 standalone)

---

## Interfaces

- **Inbound:** Retrieval API (REST) — accepts query, filters, and top-k; Ingestion API — accepts documents for processing
- **Outbound:** Vector stores (OpenSearch, Aurora pgvector), Bedrock/LLM Gateway for embeddings and generation, S3 for document storage

---

## Interoperability

| Relationship    | References                                                                                              |
|-----------------|---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| **Consumed by** | Agents (SBB-AGT-001, SBB-AGT-002), Prompt Engineering (SBB-CORE-001, SBB-CORE-002), domain applications |
| **Works with**  | AWS Bedrock (SBB-INTG-001), LLM Gateway (SBB-INTG-003), Self-Hosted Embedding (SBB-INTG-005), Bedrock Guardrails (SBB-XCUT-001), ApplyGuardrail API (SBB-XCUT-002) |

---

## Dependent Building Blocks

- SBB-INTG-001 (AWS Bedrock) or SBB-INTG-003 (LLM Gateway) — LLM for generation and embedding
- SBB-INTG-005 (Self-Hosted Embedding) — optional self-hosted embedding alternative

---

## SBB Mapping

- Runtime: Amazon EKS or AWS Lambda
- Vector Store: OpenSearch (k-NN) or Aurora PostgreSQL (pgvector)
- Document Store: Amazon S3
- LLM & Embedding: AWS Bedrock or LLM Gateway (LiteLLM)

name: Bedrock Knowledge Bases
markdown_content:
# SBB-KR-002

| Field       | Value                                       |
|-------------|---------------------------------------------|
| **ID**      | SBB-KR-002                                  |
| **Name**    | Bedrock Knowledge Bases                     |
| **Version** | 1.0.0                                       |
| **Status**  | DRAFT                                       |
| **ABB Ref** | ABB-KR-001 (Knowledge & Retrieval)          |
| **Owner**   | Enterprise AI Architect                     |

---

## Specific Functionality

AWS managed RAG solution via Bedrock Knowledge Bases. Automatic document processing pipeline with zero infrastructure management. For teams that need RAG capability without building custom ingestion and retrieval pipelines. Includes:

- Automatic ingestion pipeline — connect S3 data sources; Bedrock handles document parsing, chunking, embedding, and indexing automatically
- Managed chunking and embedding — configurable chunking strategies (fixed-size, semantic) with Bedrock embedding models (Titan, Cohere)
- Multiple vector store backends — OpenSearch Serverless, Aurora PostgreSQL, Pinecone, or Redis Enterprise as managed vector stores
- Hybrid retrieval — combine semantic search with metadata filtering for scoped, accurate retrieval
- RetrieveAndGenerate API — single API call that retrieves relevant chunks and generates a grounded response with source citations
- Data source sync — scheduled or on-demand synchronization to keep knowledge bases current with source document changes
- Native agent integration — Bedrock AgentCore (SBB-AGT-001) connects to Knowledge Bases natively for retrieval-augmented reasoning

---

## Interfaces

- **Inbound:** Bedrock Retrieve API, RetrieveAndGenerate API
- **Outbound:** S3 (document source), managed vector stores, Bedrock models (embedding and generation)

---

## Interoperability

| Relationship    | References                                                                         |
|-----------------|------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| **Consumed by** | Bedrock AgentCore (SBB-AGT-001), Custom Agents (SBB-AGT-002), domain applications |
| **Works with**  | AWS Bedrock (SBB-INTG-001), Bedrock Guardrails (SBB-XCUT-001)                     |

---

## Dependent Building Blocks

- SBB-INTG-001 (AWS Bedrock) — embedding and generation models

---

## SBB Mapping

- Runtime: AWS Managed (Bedrock Knowledge Bases)
- Data Source: Amazon S3
- Vector Store: OpenSearch Serverless, Aurora PostgreSQL, Pinecone, or Redis
- LLM & Embedding: AWS Bedrock (Titan Embeddings, Claude)

name: Amazon Kendra
markdown_content:
# SBB-KR-003

| Field       | Value                                       |
|-------------|---------------------------------------------|
| **ID**      | SBB-KR-003                                  |
| **Name**    | Amazon Kendra                               |
| **Version** | 1.0.0                                       |
| **Status**  | DRAFT                                       |
| **ABB Ref** | ABB-KR-001 (Knowledge & Retrieval)          |
| **Owner**   | Enterprise AI Architect                     |

---

## Specific Functionality

AWS managed enterprise search service with 40+ native data source connectors. For organizations that need a unified search layer across multiple enterprise systems without building custom ingestion pipelines for each source. Includes:

- 40+ native connectors — pre-built connectors for SharePoint, Salesforce, ServiceNow, S3, RDS, Confluence, Jira, and more; no custom ETL code
- Semantic search — ML-powered relevance ranking that understands natural language queries, not just keyword matching
- Incremental crawling — connectors detect changes in source systems and update the index incrementally without full re-ingestion
- Access control inheritance — Kendra respects source system permissions (ACLs); users only see results they're authorized to access
- Custom document enrichment — Lambda-based preprocessing to enrich documents with metadata, classifications, or transformations before indexing
- RAG integration — Retrieve API returns relevant passages that can be injected as context into LLM prompts; works alongside Bedrock Knowledge Bases (SBB-KR-002) for hybrid retrieval strategies

---

## Interfaces

- **Inbound:** Kendra Query API, Retrieve API
- **Outbound:** Source system connectors (SharePoint, Salesforce, S3, databases, etc.)

---

## Interoperability

| Relationship    | References                                                                                    |
|-----------------|-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| **Consumed by** | Agents (SBB-AGT-001, SBB-AGT-002), Custom RAG (SBB-KR-001), domain applications             |
| **Works with**  | Bedrock Knowledge Bases (SBB-KR-002), Bedrock Guardrails (SBB-XCUT-001)                      |

---

## Dependent Building Blocks

None — AWS managed service

---

## SBB Mapping

- Runtime: AWS Managed (Amazon Kendra)
- Connectors: 40+ native connectors (SharePoint, Salesforce, S3, ServiceNow, Confluence, etc.)
- Search: Semantic + keyword hybrid

name: Bedrock Guardrails (Integrated)
markdown_content:
# SBB-XCUT-001

| Field       | Value                                     |
|-------------|-------------------------------------------|
| **ID**      | SBB-XCUT-001                              |
| **Name**    | Bedrock Guardrails (Integrated)           |
| **Version** | 1.0.0                                     |
| **Status**  | DRAFT                                     |
| **ABB Ref** | ABB-XCUT-001 (AI Guardrails)             |
| **Owner**   | Enterprise AI Architect                   |

---

## Specific Functionality

Integrated guardrails for AWS Bedrock LLM calls. Guardrails are applied automatically during model invocation via the guardrailIdentifier parameter — no separate API call required. This is the lowest-latency guardrail option for services that call Bedrock directly. Includes:

- Inline enforcement — guardrails evaluated as part of the InvokeModel / Converse API call; single round-trip for inference + safety check
- Content filters — configurable thresholds for hate, insults, sexual content, violence, and misconduct across both input and output
- Denied topics — block specific topics (e.g., competitor advice, investment recommendations) with natural language topic definitions
- Word filters — block profanity and custom word/phrase lists
- PII detection — identify and redact or block personally identifiable information (names, SSN, email, phone, etc.)
- Contextual grounding — detect hallucinations by comparing model output against reference context (retrieval sources)
- Guardrail versioning — version-controlled guardrail configurations with DRAFT and active versions for safe rollout

---

## Interfaces

- **Integrated mode:** guardrailIdentifier and guardrailVersion parameters on Bedrock InvokeModel / Converse API
- **Limitation:** Only available for services calling Bedrock directly; not applicable when LLM calls route through LLM Gateway (SBB-INTG-003)

---

## Interoperability

| Relationship    | References                                                                                              |
|-----------------|---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| **Consumed by** | Prompt Engineering — Direct (SBB-CORE-001, SBB-CORE-003), Bedrock AgentCore (SBB-AGT-001), Bedrock Knowledge Bases (SBB-KR-002) |
| **Works with**  | AWS Bedrock (SBB-INTG-001)                                                                              |

---

## Dependent Building Blocks

- SBB-INTG-001 (AWS Bedrock) — guardrails are a Bedrock-native feature

---

## SBB Mapping

- Runtime: AWS Managed (part of Bedrock)
- Guardrails: AWS Bedrock Guardrails (integrated mode)
- LLM: AWS Bedrock only

name: Bedrock ApplyGuardrail API (Standalone)
markdown_content:
# SBB-XCUT-002

| Field       | Value                                     |
|-------------|-------------------------------------------|
| **ID**      | SBB-XCUT-002                              |
| **Name**    | Bedrock ApplyGuardrail API (Standalone)   |
| **Version** | 1.0.0                                     |
| **Status**  | DRAFT                                     |
| **ABB Ref** | ABB-XCUT-001 (AI Guardrails)             |
| **Owner**   | Enterprise AI Architect                   |

---

## Specific Functionality

Standalone guardrails for any LLM provider. Uses the Bedrock ApplyGuardrail API to validate input and output independently of model invocation — decoupled from the LLM call itself. This is the required guardrail option for services that access models through the LLM Gateway (SBB-INTG-003) or external providers. Includes:

- Provider-agnostic enforcement — validate input/output for any LLM: Bedrock via LLM Gateway, Azure OpenAI, self-hosted models (vLLM, TGI), or external APIs
- Pre-call input validation — call ApplyGuardrail before sending prompt to model; block or modify input that violates policy
- Post-call output validation — call ApplyGuardrail after receiving model response; filter or block output before returning to consumer
- Same guardrail configurations — uses the same guardrail definitions (content filters, denied topics, word filters, PII) as integrated mode (SBB-XCUT-001); single policy definition, two enforcement modes
- Batch evaluation — validate multiple text blocks in a single API call for efficient pipeline processing
- Audit logging — every ApplyGuardrail evaluation (action taken, violations detected) logged for compliance

---

## Interfaces

- **Inbound:** Bedrock ApplyGuardrail API — accepts text content and guardrail ID/version; returns PASS or INTERVENED with modified content
- **Outbound:** None — stateless evaluation service

---

## Interoperability

| Relationship    | References                                                                                              |
|-----------------|---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| **Consumed by** | Prompt Engineering — Gateway-Routed (SBB-CORE-002), Prompt Engineering — Lambda (SBB-CORE-003), Custom Agents (SBB-AGT-002), Custom RAG (SBB-KR-001) |
| **Works with**  | LLM Gateway (SBB-INTG-003), Self-Hosted LLM/SLM (SBB-INTG-004), any external LLM provider            |

---

## Dependent Building Blocks

None — AWS managed API (uses Bedrock guardrail definitions but does not require Bedrock model invocation)

---

## SBB Mapping

- Runtime: AWS Managed (Bedrock API)
- Guardrails: AWS Bedrock ApplyGuardrail API (standalone mode)
- LLM: Any (provider-agnostic)
